items.title: Metacognition in Decision-Making Across Domains and Modalities: Evidence from Three Studies
items.author: Audrey Mazancieux, Katarzyna Hat, Renate Rutiku, Michał Wierzchoń, Kristian Sandberg
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Metacognition involves second-order judgments about first-order judgments. It remains unclear whether an individual’s confidence in being correct is generated by the same system across tasks (domain generality) or whether it is computed independently in ...</article>

items.title: Detection of Idiosyncratic Gaze-Fingerprint Signatures in Humans
items.author: Sarah K. Crockford, Eleonora Satta, Ines Severino, Donatella Fiacchino, Andrea Vitale, Natasha Bertelsen, Elena Maria Busuoli, Veronica Mandelli, Michael V. Lombardo
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Do individuals possess a “gaze fingerprint” that reveals how they uniquely look at the world? We tested this question by examining intra- and intersubject gaze similarity across 700 static pictures of complex natural scenes. Independent discovery (n= 105)...</article>

items.title: Pretending Not to Know Reveals a Capacity for Model-Based Self-Simulation
items.author: Matan Mazor, Chaz Firestone, Ian Phillips
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Pretending not to know requires appreciating how one would behave without a given piece of knowledge and acting accordingly. Here, two game-based experiments reveal a capacity to simulate decision-making under such counterfactual ignorance. English-...</article>

items.title: Fidelity Versus Validity Using Anendophasia as an Example: Commentary on Nedergaard and Lupyan (2024) and Lind (2025)
items.author: Russell T. HurlburtDepartment of Psychology, University of Nevada, Las Vegas
items.content: <article>Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Nedergaard and Lupyan (2024) presented four studies aimed at validating anendophasia (i.e., experiencing no inner speech).1However, Lind (2025) held that no one, including Nedergaard and Lupyan, has demonstrated that anendophasia exists. In both articles,...</article>
